evidence: We are investing in artificial intelligence (AI) across the entire company and infusing generative AI capabilities into our consumer and commercial offerings.
We will bear significant development and operational costs to build and support the AI models, services, platforms, and infrastructure necessary to meet the needs of our customers.
We make significant investments in research, development, and marketing for existing products, services, and technologies, including AI-based products and services.
Azure AI Foundry is a unified platform for developers to design, customize, and manage AI applications and agents.
Microsoft 365 Commercial is an AI-powered business and productivity solutions platform that brings together Office, Windows, Microsoft 365 Copilot, and Enterprise Mobility + Security.

Nuance Healthcare provides AI solutions to the healthcare industry. GitHub provides a collaboration platform for developers to manage code and incorporate AI and agent-based tools across the software development lifecycle.

AI algorithms or training methodologies may be flawed. Datasets may be overbroad, insufficient, or contain biased or inaccurate information. Content generated by AI systems may be offensive, illegal, inaccurate, or otherwise harmful.

Our implementation of AI systems could result in legal liability, regulatory action, brand, reputational, or competitive harm... risks may stem from issues related to intellectual property, data privacy, and other claims associated with AI training and outputs.
Increasing use of generative AI models in our internal systems may create new attack surfaces or methods for adversaries... features that rely on generative AI can be susceptible to security threats.

capex_mentions.quote: We are incurring significant costs to build and maintain infrastructure to support cloud-based and AI services, reducing operating margins.
capex_mentions.context: Risk factors discussion of cloud and AI infrastructure investment costs

capex_mentions.quote: We spend substantial am[ounts]... Our increasing user traffic, growth in services, and the complexity of our products and services demand more computing power.
capex_mentions.context: Operations section discussing compute infrastructure spend for AI services

limitations: No specific dollar amounts or quantified capex/R&D figures for AI investment appear in the provided excerpts; investment intensity scored 4 rather than 5 due to absence of quantified multi-year commitment figures in these excerpts.

Narrative concreteness scored 4 (not 5) because no quantified business results (%, $, time saved) for deployed AI use cases appear in the excerpts; named products are described as deployed but without outcome metrics.
Excerpts appear truncated in several places (e.g., 'We spend substantial am'); full filing may contain additional quantitative disclosures not visible here.

AI risk disclosure depth scored 4 rather than 5 because no named governance oversight bodies, quantified risk exposure, or formal mitigation programs are cited in the excerpts, though risk mechanisms are described with specificity.
